title: When Code and Creativity Collide
gist: A WEF Agenda Dialogues podcast landing page for a Davos 2026 session on AI's intersection with music and human creativity, featuring Harvey Mason jr. (Recording Academy) and will.i.am. NOTE: the captured text is almost entirely cookie-consent boilerplate; the only substantive content is the episode blurb, so this is a light card.

insights.claim: A WEF Davos 2026 panel with the Recording Academy's Harvey Mason jr. and musician will.i.am framed AI as 'remixing the rules of music' and raised whether a machine that can craft a hit erodes what only humans can create, foregrounding authorship and equity questions for the music industry.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Authorship and royalty/equity disputes around AI-generated music are a building regulatory and litigation risk for labels, streaming platforms and rights organizations.
insights.quote: AI is remixing the rules of music and the role of the human artist. If a machine can craft a hit, what's left that only humans can create?
